stories.headline: Brazil's former spy chief detained by ICE agents in US

stories.headline: Katrina Dudley on Oil Prices, Market Impact of War In Iran
stories.summary: Katrina Dudley, senior investment strategist at Franklin Templeton, discusses energy prices and market impacts of the war in Iran. She speaks with Matt Miller on "The Brief." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: How much will US Hormuz blockade hurt Iran, and does Tehran have an escape?
stories.summary: Iran earned $5bn in oil exports in past month while it shut the strait for most other ships. That revenue is at risk.

stories.headline: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?

stories.headline: Lagarde Says Early ECB Exit No Option With Clouds on Horizon
stories.summary: President Christine Lagarde said an early exit from the European Central Bank isn’t currently an option for her in the face of the economic crisis brought on by the Iran war.

stories.headline: Warsh Fed Chair Hearing Set for Next Week, Tim Scott Says
stories.summary: The Senate Banking Committee will hold a confirmation hearing next week for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair, the panel’s top Republican said Tuesday.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell Says He Will Resign From Congress After Sex Abuse Accusations
stories.summary: The California lawmaker said in a social media post Monday that he would quit. He has denied accounts of abusing women.

stories.headline: Accusers seek justice after unwanted explicit messages from Congressman Eric Swalwell
stories.summary: He denies claims of sexual assault but admits " mistakes" and says he will resign.

stories.headline: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

stories.headline: United CEO Proposes American Airlines Merger
stories.summary: A shocking airline mega-merger proposal could reshape global aviation. Bloomberg's Benedikt Kammel joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down United’s bold pitch to merge with American Airlines, why crisis conditions are driving consolidation, and why US travelers pay far more than Europeans for flights. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ECB's Lagarde on Iran War's Economic Impact, Rates and AI
stories.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde discusses the impact of the war in Iran and AI on the euro-zone economy with Bloomberg's Francine Lacqua at the IMF’s spring meetings. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Analyst Warns That Oil Prices Threaten Banks
stories.summary: JP Morgan’s earnings look strong, but the real story is beneath the surface says Baird Senior Research Analyst David George. He joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why net interest income fears are overblown, why trading gains won’t last, and the one macro risk investors should actually watch: rising oil prices and the ripple effect on consumers and corporate margins. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Lagarde Says Europe’s Economy Has Slipped Below ECB Baseline
stories.summary: President Christine Lagarde said higher energy costs have pushed the euro zone away from the European Central Bank’s base-case outlook, though not enough yet to warrant leaning toward raising interest rates.

stories.headline: Lagarde Says EU Economy Between ECB Baseline, Adverse Scenarios
stories.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde says the euro-zone economy has moved away from the central bank's Iran war base case, though not enough to currently warrant leaning toward raising interest rates. She speaks with Bloomberg's Francine Lacqua at the IMF’s spring meetings. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank’s Saravelos Says Sell Dollar as War Risks Peaked
stories.summary: The risks linked to the war in Iran will be subsiding and it is time to bet on a weaker US dollar, according to Deutsche Bank AG.

stories.headline: Cocoa Price Crash Spurs Hopes for Demand Recovery, Cheaper Candy
stories.summary: The collapse in cocoa futures is stoking optimism among some industry players that the pace of processing will improve as cheaper beans filter through the supply chain, easing the price of candy bars.

stories.headline: Market ‘Sugar High’ Will Send Stocks to Record, Wells Fargo Says
stories.summary: The war in Iran may be ongoing, but traders have largely moved on, as a number of bullish catalysts in the coming months are poised to propel the S&P 500 Index to new highs, according to Wells Fargo Securities.

stories.headline: Trump and MAGA Embraced Orban. Now, They’re Worried by His Loss in Hungary’s Election.
stories.summary: Viktor Orban, the Hungarian prime minister, and his right-wing playbook were embraced by parts of the American right. Now some are worried by his defeat.

stories.headline: ‘We are a stable, predictable country’: Spanish PM Sanchez in Beijing
stories.summary: Spain's Pedro Sánchez and China's Xi Jinping stressed respect for international law and a shared “sense of justice”.

stories.summary: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

stories.summary: The U.S. military has provided few details on how it might carry out President Trump’s orders as he seeks to pressure Tehran on a peace deal. But history and established practices offer some clues.

stories.headline: As Iran war drags on, midterm forecasts for Republicans get even worse
stories.summary: The GOP was already bracing for a tough election, but with the ceasefire in doubt, gas prices are likely to stay high, and voters’ economic outlook is dismal.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
stories.summary: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

stories.headline: Prosecutors Investigate ICE Agents Who Detained ChongLy Scott Thao in Minnesota
stories.summary: ChongLy Scott Thao, a naturalized U.S. citizen, was briefly detained during a federal immigration crackdown in Minnesota in January.

stories.headline: Trump’s Explanation for an Image of Himself as Jesus: ‘I Thought It Was Me as a Doctor’
stories.summary: The image showed President Trump bathed in divine light and clad in religious robes. His interpretation was that the image depicted him as a doctor, not Jesus Christ.

stories.headline: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

stories.headline: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.headline: Why Real Madrid tie could make or break Kane's Ballon d'Or hopes
stories.summary: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?
